permit_information.approach_other: Gravel residential driveway approach with 18" culvert under new access to single-family home
permit_information.encroachment_other: Minor grading within the county right-of-way to blend the new driveway approach into the existing gravel shoulder and roadside ditch

applicant_or_permittee.current_use_of_property: Vacant 2.1-acre residential lot with natural sagebrush and native grasses. No existing structures or driveways; currently used only for occasional parking when we hike or bike in the area and for staging building materials while we work through permits for a future single-family residence.

property__project_information.physical_location_of_project: 13285 East Glendale Road, approximately 0.4 miles east of the intersection with Buttercup Road, on the south side of East Glendale Road, Hailey, Blaine County, ID 83333
property__project_information.legal_description: Lot 7, Block 3, Glendale Bench Subdivision No. 2, located in Section 22, Township 2 North, Range 18 East, Boise Meridian, Blaine County, Idaho
property__project_information.estimate_provided_by: Sawtooth Excavation & Grading, LLC (Michael J. Larson, Owner)
property__project_information.zoning_of_project_area: R-1 (Low Density Residential) per Blaine County GIS
property__project_information.project_duration: Approximately 5 working days on-site spread over a 2-week period, weather permitting
property__project_information.project_narrative_line_1: The proposed project consists of constructing a new residential driveway approach connecting a future single-family home site on Lot 7, Block 3 of Glendale Bench Subdivision No. 2 to East Glendale Road. Work within the Blaine County right-of-way will include minor grading of the existing gravel shoulder, excavation of a shallow trench across the roadside ditch, installation of an 18-inch diameter corrugated HDPE culvert (approximately 28 feet in length) to maintain ditch drainage, placement and compaction of a gravel driving surface, and shaping of side slopes to tie into existing grades. The driveway will be approximately 16 feet wide at the property line and will flare to about 24 feet at the edge of the county road to allow safe turning movements for passenger vehicles, delivery trucks, and emergency vehicles. All work is intended to comply with Blaine County Road & Bridge standards and to preserve existing drainage patterns along East Glendale Road.
property__project_information.project_narrative_line_2: Construction is planned to begin shortly after permit approval, tentatively the week of 2025-05-12, and is expected to take no more than 5 working days on site, with the majority of excavation and culvert installation completed in the first 2 days. Sawtooth Excavation & Grading, LLC will provide all labor, equipment, and materials, including traffic control measures such as advance warning signs and flaggers if needed during short-duration lane encroachments. Work will be scheduled between 8:00 AM and 5:00 PM on weekdays only to minimize impacts on local traffic. Following installation, disturbed areas within the right-of-way will be re-graded and compacted, and any excess material will be hauled off site. The driveway approach is being constructed now to allow construction access for a home we plan to build in 2026; until home construction begins, the driveway will be used only for occasional access to the lot and will remain graveled (no paving is proposed at this time).

application_attachments.other_application_attachments: 1) Vicinity map showing the location of Lot 7 relative to East Glendale Road and Buttercup Road. 2) Two color photographs of the existing roadside ditch and shoulder where the driveway is proposed, taken from both eastbound and westbound directions. 3) Manufacturer’s specification sheet for the 18" corrugated HDPE culvert pipe, including load ratings and installation recommendations. 4) Copy of the recorded plat for Glendale Bench Subdivision No. 2 highlighting Lot 7 and adjacent right-of-way. 5) Email correspondence from Blaine County Land Use & Building Services confirming zoning (R-1) and indicating that a separate building permit will be required prior to home construction.
